overview: Google Guest Agent could allow unintended access to network services.
description:
USN-7839-1 fixed vulnerabilities in Go Cryptography. This update provides
the corresponding update in the Go Cryptography module included in Google
Guest Agent.

Original advisory details:

Damien Tournoud, Patrick Dawkins, Vince Parker, and Jules Duvivier
 discovered that Go Cryptography incorrectly handled public keys during SSH
 operations. An attacker could possibly use this issue to bypass
 authorization mechanisms.

 

 

 

USN-7839-1 fixed vulnerabilities in Go Cryptography. This update provides
the corresponding update in the Go Cryptography module included in Google
Guest Agent.

Original advisory details:

Damien Tournoud, Patrick Dawkins, Vince Parker, and Jules Duvivier
 discovered that Go Cryptography incorrectly handled public keys during SSH
 operations. An attacker could possibly use this issue to bypass
 authorization mechanisms.
full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-7839-2

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-7839-2: Google Guest Agent vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
3 November 2025

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
Google Guest Agent could allow unintended access to network services.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 25.04

 24.04 LTS

22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

18.04 LTS

16.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 google-guest-agent - Google Compute Engine Guest Agent
 

 

 
Details

 

 

USN-7839-1 fixed vulnerabilities in Go Cryptography. This update provides
the corresponding update in the Go Cryptography module included in Google
Guest Agent.

Original advisory details:

Damien Tournoud, Patrick Dawkins, Vince Parker, and Jules Duvivier
 discovered that Go Cryptography incorrectly handled public keys during SSH
 operations. An attacker could possibly use this issue to bypass
 authorization mechanisms.

 

 

 

USN-7839-1 fixed vulnerabilities in Go Cryptography. This update provides
the corresponding update in the Go Cryptography module included in Google
Guest Agent.

Original advisory details:

Damien Tournoud, Patrick Dawkins, Vince Parker, and Jules Duvivier
 discovered that Go Cryptography incorrectly handled public keys during SSH
 operations. An attacker could possibly use this issue to bypass
 authorization mechanisms.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 25.04

plucky

 google-guest-agent – 

20250116.00-0ubuntu2.1

 

 24.04

LTS noble

 google-guest-agent – 

20250116.00-0ubuntu1~24.04.2

 

 22.04

LTS jammy

 google-guest-agent – 

20250116.00-0ubuntu1~22.04.1

 

 20.04

LTS focal

 google-guest-agent – 

20250116.00-0ubuntu1~20.04.0+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro.

 

 18.04

LTS bionic

 google-guest-agent – 

20241011.01-0ubuntu1~18.04.0+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

 16.04

LTS xenial

 google-guest-agent – 

20240716.00-0ubuntu1~16.04.0+esm1

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.

 

 

Reduce your security exposure

 

 Ubuntu Pro provides ten-year security coverage to 25,000+ packages in Main and Universe repositories, and it is free for up to five machines.
 

 

 Get Ubuntu Pro
